summary: Crystal‑clear 1971 soundboard captures a raw, high‑energy show highlighted by an epic Dark Star → Bird Song and stellar China Cat → I Know You Rider sequence.
review: The Miller‑sourced soundboard restoration is excellent – crisp, well‑balanced and virtually free of tape artifacts. Musically the concert is a standout early‑’71 performance: a soaring Dark Star into Bird Song, a blistering China Cat > I Know You Rider, plus memorable Uncle John’s Band and Johnny B. Goode moments, all fueled by Jerry’s fiery guitar, Phil’s inventive bass and Bill Kreutzmann’s thunderous drumming.

ai_rating.rationale: Ten detailed reviews overwhelmingly praise both the show (exceptional jams, high energy) and the recording (restored soundboard, clear mix). The weighted average of show quality (70%) and recording quality (30%) yields a near‑perfect score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Restored from 1971 tape by Charlie Miller; crisp, balanced mix with minimal hiss or dropouts. Listeners note the recording captures Bill’s drums and Jerry’s guitar brilliantly, with only faint tape artifacts.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set starts a bit slow but builds into the spectacular Dark Star → Bird Song sequence; second set maintains momentum with China Cat > Rider, Uncle John’s Band and a rousing encore. The raw early‑’71 energy and the infamous Yale Bowl riot add a unique, electric atmosphere.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for fiery guitar tone, on‑stage jokes, and trad lead work on Johnny B. Goode.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for inventive bass runs in Dark Star and China Cat, captured well on the Miller source.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pig (organ) noted walking around the stage; less psychedelic but still part of the jam texture.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann described as 'out of control' with massive fills that are beautifully recorded.
